page: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is a virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 300 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. In contrast, multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU scores, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also register average performance.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is structured for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Qualitative cost efficiency is driven by the high single-core CPU performance and bundled 300 GB SSD storage, though the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for sustained multi-core execution. It is suitable for web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale compilation tasks, while showing poor performance in specific low-parameter LLM text generation tasks.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a bundled 300 GB local SSD. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU execution, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the average tier. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a compute-oriented profile. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, moderate database operations, and single-threaded processing tasks, presenting a balanced resource density for applications that benefit from strong single-core performance without requiring high memory-to-CPU ratios.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 300 GB SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-16xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and 300 GB SSD storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-oriented virtual server featuring sixteen AMD EPYC cores, bundled SSD storage, and exceptional single-threaded processing performance.
bullet_points: 16 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
32.0 GB RAM providing 2.0 GB of memory per core
Bundled 300 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database execution
Optimized for compute-focused workloads, web serving, and utility databases
